data.title: Bitcoin's value increased by 286% in 2021, then declined 42% in 2022. It recovered by 52% in 2023, surging to nearly $57,000 by 2024.
data.description: This year, the United States celebrates its 249th anniversary on the Fourth of July. Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC), known for its principles of financial independence, has experienced notable changes in recent years around this holiday. In the period between 2020 and 2021, the value of Bitcoin surged by an impressive 286%. However, the bear market of 2022 led to a sharp 42% decline in its price. Following this, Bitcoin started a steady upward trend, rebounding by 52% in 2023 and reaching nearly $57,000 by the last Independence Day. The last quarter of 2024 and the first half of 2025 also showed positive... Find further details on the complete story available on Benzinga.com

data.title: Itaú Chile launches Sustainable Finance Framework, favorably assessed by S&P Global Ratings. Comprehensive platform for green, social, and sustainability-linked instruments.
data.description: BANCO ITAÚ CHILE (SSE by nuam: ITAUCL) unveiled its inaugural Sustainable Finance Framework in Santiago, Chile on July 04, 2025. This initiative marks the bank's strategic move towards promoting environmentally sound, socially responsible, and sustainable financial practices. The Framework introduces a robust system for launching eco-friendly, socially beneficial, and sustainability-driven financial products in compliance with global best practices.

data.title: DOGE shows double-bottom pattern, suggesting price bounce to $0.25.
data.description: A new study has reported an alarming increase in obesity rates, with the number of obese individuals reaching 650 million worldwide. The research found that this rise is particularly prominent in developed countries, where 13% of the global population resides but accounts for 60% of the world's obese population. Furthermore, the study highlighted that obesity-related diseases have also surged, with an estimated 2.8 million deaths annually directly attributed to excess weight. These findings underscore the urgent need for comprehensive strategies to tackle the global obesity epidemic.

data.title: Azumah Resources to start construction of Black Volta Gold Mine in Ghana, marking a new era for mining sector with local and international investors.
data.description: Ghana-based Azumah Resources Ghana Limited has initiated construction of the Black Volta Gold Mine in the Upper West Region of Ghana. This important development signifies a significant step forward for the country's mining industry. Azumah Resources has been overseen exclusively by Ghanaians and has received support from dedicated local and global stakeholders. The project funding was secured through current shareholders of Azumah Resources. The commencement of the mine's construction is a key achievement for the company and the sector overall.

data.title: Minister Al-Jasser launches logistics corridor linking Jeddah Port and Al-Khumrah Park, boosting infrastructure.
data.description: On July 4, 2025, in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, the Minister of Transport and Logistic Services and Chairman of the Saudi Ports Authority (Mawani), Eng. Saleh bin Nasser Al-Jasser, initiated a significant initiative to enhance the country's logistics infrastructure. This move aims to boost the efficiency and capacity of Saudi Arabia's ports to handle a projected increase in trade volume. The Minister's actions are part of a broader strategy to modernize the transportation sector and align it with global standards. This development holds promise for fostering economic growth and attracting more investments into the country, ultimately contributing to job creation and prosperity.

China’s aircraft carrier Shandong arrived in on Thursday for a five-day visit, opening its decks to the public. It received a warm welcome, with some spectators gathering along the shoreline with binoculars and zoom lenses to catch a distant glimpse.
